title: Baked Tuna Egg Mayo on Japanese Rice with Teriyaki Sauce
summary: Tuna mayo but make it baked, golden and bubbling. Layered over sticky Japanese rice and finished with a drizzle of glossy teriyaki sauce. So wrong, it’s right.

ingredients.label: 400g cooked Japanese or short grain rice
ingredients.name: Cooked Japanese Rice

method.description: Boil the eggs. Bring a saucepan of water to a boil. Carefully lower in the eggs and cook for 6 mins. While they cook, prep a bowl of cold water. Once done, transfer the eggs to the cold water and let them sit for 5 mins. Peel and cut into quarters.

Prep the tuna mix. Thinly slice the spring onion. Finely mince the brown onion. Drain the tuna and add to a mixing bowl along with the spring onion, brown onion, Japanese mayo, sriracha, soy sauce, and black pepper. Mix gently to combine, keeping some tuna in chunks.
Fold the egg quarters into the tuna mixture gently to avoid breaking them up too much.
Spoon the cooked rice into a small-medium ovenproof dish and spread evenly. Top with the tuna egg mayo mix and smooth out.
Turn your grill to high. Place the dish on the middle shelf and cook for 10 mins, or until golden, slightly crispy and bubbling on top.
Peel and grate the ginger. Add to a small pan with the soy sauce, mirin, sake (if using), and sugar. Stir to combine and bring to a boil. Let it bubble for 1 minute, then remove from the heat.
Remove the bake from the oven. Drizzle over the teriyaki sauce. Finely chop the chives and sprinkle on top along with the toasted sesame seeds. Serve hot.

tips: Great for meal prep. Make and chill in the fridge for up to 3 days. Reheat in a 180°C oven until warmed through.
Store the sauce separately for max freshness.
Not suitable for freezing due to mayo and egg texture change.
Not recommended – the mayo and egg don’t reheat well once frozen

chefs.name: Justin Tsang
chefs.summary: Justin ‘Dustbin’ Tsang is a cook specialising in indulgent, home-cooked Asian food with a twist. Inspired by his upbringing around his parents Chinese restaurants, he shares his knowledge on cooking techniques and making Asian cooking more exciting and accessible.
